title: scenario_ev_fire
description: Electric vehicle battery fire on a highway with thermal runaway risk

timeline.title: Dispatch
timeline.description: Report of a smoking electric vehicle on the highway median. Caller states the vehicle is not on fire yet but emitting thick white smoke.

timeline.title: En Route
timeline.description: Dispatch confirms the EV is now showing visible flames from the undercarriage. Hazmat team notified. Driver has self-evacuated.

timeline.title: On Scene
timeline.description: Tesla sedan in median with orange flames erupting from battery pack. Intense white smoke. Driver standing 30 feet away. HOV lane blocked.

timeline.title: Thermal Runaway
timeline.description: Battery pack cells venting audibly. Temperature readings near 800°C at pack surface. Reignition cycle beginning. Flames pulse in and out every 30 seconds.

timeline.title: Mass Water Application
timeline.description: 2.5-inch line applying 300 GPM directly into battery pack area. Thermal camera shows cooling beginning in outer cells.

timeline.title: Fire Suppressed
timeline.description: Active flames suppressed after 45 minutes of continuous water application. Vehicle moved to containment area for extended monitoring.

node-1.title: Initial Size-Up
node-1.situation: You arrive to find a late-model electric sedan in the highway median with orange flames erupting from the undercarriage. White toxic smoke is billowing. The driver is self-evacuated and uninjured 30 feet upwind. Wind is pushing smoke toward the travel lanes. Traffic is backing up.
node-1.question: What is your immediate priority action?

options.label: A
options.text: Establish a 150-foot perimeter upwind, request hazmat response and additional water supply, don SCBA before approaching, and identify the vehicle make and battery type from the manufacturer's emergency response guide.

options.label: B
options.text: Apply a dry chemical extinguisher to the battery compartment to quickly knock down the fire and reopen the highway.

options.label: C
options.text: Disconnect the 12V auxiliary battery under the hood to cut power to the high-voltage battery management system before applying water.

node-2.title: Water Application Strategy
node-2.situation: Hazmat is 15 minutes out. The battery pack is in full thermal runaway—cells are venting and reigniting. Your ERG and manufacturer guide indicate the pack requires continuous high-volume water immersion. You have one 2.5-inch line available producing 300 GPM. The undercarriage is inaccessible without breaching the battery enclosure.
node-2.question: How do you attack the battery fire?

options.label: A
options.text: Apply continuous high-volume water (300 GPM) directly into the undercarriage battery area from a safe distance, accepting that suppression will take 30–60 minutes and prepare for multiple reignition cycles.

options.label: B
options.text: Attempt to pierce the battery enclosure with a TIC-guided drill to inject water directly into cells for faster suppression.

options.label: C
options.text: Cover the vehicle with a fire blanket to smother the battery fire and conserve water.

node-3.title: Reignition Management
node-3.situation: After 20 minutes of water application, the flames appear knocked down but the battery pack reignites twice within 5 minutes. Hazmat has arrived. Thermal imaging shows two cell modules still above 600°C. The highway authority is demanding the road be cleared. Your water supply is adequate but the tanker needs to relay.
node-3.question: What do you order?

options.label: A
options.text: Maintain continuous water application, coordinate a tanker relay to sustain supply, advise the highway authority the scene cannot be cleared prematurely due to reignition risk, and arrange post-suppression vehicle transport to a quarantine area.

options.label: B
options.text: Declare the fire suppressed after the visible flames go out and release the scene to allow traffic to resume.

options.label: C
options.text: Apply foam concentrate over the battery area to create a vapor seal and end the water application.

node-4.title: Scenario Complete
node-4.situation: After 50 minutes of sustained high-volume water application, the battery pack temperature dropped below 200°C and reignition ceased. The vehicle was transported to a designated impound lot for 72-hour monitoring per manufacturer protocol. SCBA use throughout the operation protected crews from toxic cell off-gases. Hazmat documented the runaway event. Your correct tactical approach—high-volume water, perimeter control, and reignition awareness—demonstrated best practice for EV battery fire response.
node-4.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
